announcement.text: Post-click growth systems built for conversion →

hero.description: We help e-commerce brands convert more of the traffic they already pay for through CRO, design, development, and experimentation.

pillars.title: CRO-Driven Strategy
pillars.description: Data-led decisions, AI-assisted research, and sharper priorities that increase conversions.

pillars.title: Post-Click Design
pillars.description: Pages and funnels that continue the promise made before the click.

pillars.title: Performance Build
pillars.description: Fast, reliable experiences engineered to preserve buying intent.

pillars.title: Test. Learn. Scale.
pillars.description: Experiments turn AI-assisted ideas into proven conversion gains.

problem.title: The click is expensive. The post-click experience decides if it pays back.
paragraphs: Most brands invest heavily in traffic — ads, SEO, social, email, and now AI-assisted campaigns — then lose performance after the click. The page, message, offer, speed, trust, and checkout determine whether that attention becomes revenue.
AI is useful when you know how to use it. We use it to move faster and think wider, then filter everything through conversion strategy, UX, copy, development, and testing.
before: Campaign promise breaks on the page
problem.outcome: More of your traffic becomes revenue.

improvements.title: Every layer that turns attention into revenue.

items.title: Messaging
items.description: Sharper value propositions, offers, headlines, and CTAs that make the next action clear.

items.title: Page Design
items.description: Clear hierarchy and visual flow built to guide attention and move users toward action.

items.title: Mobile UX
items.description: Touch-first interactions and responsive journeys designed to convert on every screen.

items.title: Page Speed
items.description: Faster experiences that reduce drop-off, improve Core Web Vitals, and protect conversion.

items.title: Funnel Flow
items.description: A connected path from entry to consideration, decision, and completed action.

items.title: Conversion
items.description: Less friction, more trust, and stronger completion across every critical step.

items.title: Experiments
items.description: Focused hypotheses and A/B tests that turn customer behavior into repeatable learnings.

items.title: Analytics
items.description: Behavioral data, reliable tracking, and insights that reveal what to improve next.

services.title: Conversion Rate Optimization
services.description: Research, behavioral analysis, AI-assisted insight, and structured testing focused on turning more visits into revenue.

services.title: Landing Page Design
services.description: Campaign-specific experiences that carry the promise of the click into a persuasive buying journey.
services.outcome: Stronger message match after the click

services.title: UX/UI Design
services.description: Clear digital journeys combining visual hierarchy, usability, persuasion, and brand consistency.

services.title: Web Development
services.description: Fast, responsive, maintainable websites engineered around performance and conversion.

services.title: Shopify Development
services.description: Custom storefronts, sections, templates, integrations, and conversion systems for Shopify.

services.title: Webflow Development
services.description: Flexible, polished marketing websites that give teams speed without sacrificing quality.

services.title: A/B Testing
services.description: Hypothesis-led experiments planned, built, launched, and analyzed so ideas become evidence, not opinions.

services.title: Funnel Optimization
services.description: Every step from entry to checkout refined to protect momentum and reduce drop-off.

services.title: Copy & Messaging
services.description: Human-edited, AI-assisted messaging aligned with objections, intent, offers, and the action you need users to take.
services.outcome: Sharper reasons to trust and buy

services.title: Growth Strategy
services.description: A practical roadmap connecting business goals with design, development, and testing priorities.

services.title: Conversion Diagnosis
services.description: Behavioral data, heatmaps, recordings, and AI-assisted pattern analysis translated into a prioritized action plan.
services.outcome: Clarity on what to fix first

process.title: A continuous loop of learning and optimization.
process.description: Not a project with an end date. A system that improves with every cycle.
process.loopLabel: Every insight feeds the next cycle.

steps.title: Analyze
steps.description: Understand traffic, behavior, performance data, and the current conversion baseline.
steps.outcome: A clear view of what is really happening.

steps.title: Diagnose
steps.description: Find friction, weak messaging, UX gaps, technical issues, and missed opportunities.

steps.title: Strategize
steps.description: Define priorities, hypotheses, page structure, testing plans, and measurable goals.

steps.title: Design
steps.description: Create persuasive, clear, responsive, and brand-aligned experiences.
steps.outcome: The strategy becomes a conversion-ready experience.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Develop fast, scalable, maintainable solutions without losing the original intent.

steps.title: Test
steps.description: Validate ideas through experiments, analytics, behavioral tracking, and real user response.

steps.title: Optimize
steps.description: Use results and learning to remove more friction and improve the experience further.

steps.title: Scale
steps.description: Apply successful patterns across campaigns, pages, products, and funnels.
steps.outcome: Validated gains spread across the business.

caseStudies.title: Post-click systems built to perform.
caseStudies.description: A selection of conversion-focused experiences across e-commerce, wellness, beauty, and product-led brands.

items.headline: A clearer purchase journey built around trust and action.
items.summary: We rebuilt the post-click experience to align the offer, reduce friction, and create a more confident path from product discovery to checkout.
items.problem: Traffic was arriving, but unclear hierarchy, weak offer framing, and a fragmented purchase flow were limiting conversion.
items.solution: We clarified the message, redesigned the product experience, improved mobile UX, and connected the purchase flow with a stronger conversion system.
items.impact: A more coherent experience designed to turn paid traffic into completed purchases.

items.headline: A guided bundle experience that makes choosing feel effortless.
items.summary: We transformed product selection into a clear, interactive bundle journey designed to increase confidence and average order value.
items.problem: Customers had strong product interest, but the bundle path lacked clarity, progress feedback, and a persuasive reason to complete the set.
items.solution: We created a dynamic bundle builder with clearer savings, stronger progress cues, and a mobile-first selection experience.
items.impact: A smoother path from exploration to a complete, higher-value purchase.

items.headline: A product story redesigned to turn curiosity into conviction.
items.summary: We reshaped the landing and product experience around education, proof, and a stronger path to purchase.
items.problem: The product had a compelling story, but the page did not guide users through benefits, objections, and proof with enough clarity.
items.solution: We rebuilt the narrative, improved visual hierarchy, added stronger proof moments, and optimized the mobile purchase journey.
items.impact: A more persuasive page that explains the product and moves users toward action.

items.headline: A cleaner product system built to make comparison and choice easier.
items.summary: We redesigned the experience around product understanding, model comparison, and conversion-focused decision support.
items.problem: The product range was strong, but users had to work too hard to understand differences, benefits, and the right model for their needs.
items.solution: We introduced clearer product architecture, better comparison moments, sharper offer framing, and a more responsive buying experience.
items.impact: A more confident path from first impression to product selection.

items.headline: A collection journey rebuilt around discovery, goals, and product fit.
items.summary: We improved the collection experience to help customers navigate products with more confidence and less friction.
items.problem: The product range was broad, but filters, imagery, descriptions, and product grouping did not fully support how customers shop.
items.solution: We refined category logic, improved filtering, aligned product imagery, and created clearer discovery paths around customer goals.
items.impact: A more intuitive way to browse, compare, and find the right product.

items.headline: A single-product hero built to sell before the scroll.
items.summary: Veganic's homepage needed to turn the first viewport into a compact product pitch: clear promise, product context, ingredients, proof, trust, and a direct path to the PDP.
items.problem: As a single-product store, Veganic could not afford a decorative hero that delayed product consideration. The page needed to quickly explain what the product is, why it matters, and why the next click is worth taking.
items.solution: We structured the hero around a product-first composition, pairing a bold wellness promise with the bottle, ingredient cues, proof markers, trust badges, and a clear product CTA.
items.impact: A first viewport designed to reduce the gap between discovery, trust, product understanding, and PDP intent.

preview.headline: Turning a hero banner into a product pitch.
preview.summary: We turned Veganic's first viewport into a focused product presentation, combining the offer, ingredients, trust signals, social proof, and a direct path to the PDP.

detail.title: From decorative hero to compact product pitch.
detail.introduction: Veganic is a single-product hair care store, which means the homepage has one job: move a visitor from first impression to product consideration as quickly and confidently as possible.

sections.label: PROJECT INTRODUCTION
sections.title: A homepage built around one product decision.
sections.body: Instead of treating the hero as a brand billboard, the design makes the product, promise, proof, and CTA visible immediately. The first viewport behaves like a condensed product page above the fold.

sections.label: BRAND CONTEXT
sections.title: Natural hair care needs clarity and trust.
sections.body: For wellness and hair care shoppers, curiosity is not enough. The page has to explain what the formula does, what is inside it, why it feels credible, and where the customer should go next.

sections.label: THE CHALLENGE
sections.title: Single-product stores have less room for vague storytelling.
sections.body: If the hero only creates atmosphere, the customer still has to search for the product logic. That adds friction between ad click, homepage arrival, product understanding, and PDP intent.

sections.label: STRATEGIC INSIGHT
sections.title: The hero needed to answer PDP-level questions early.
sections.body: A single-product hero can carry more conversion responsibility than a standard banner. It should introduce the result, the product, ingredient reasons to believe, social proof, safety cues, and action in one organized composition.

sections.label: THE SOLUTION
sections.title: Product-first composition with conversion cues built in.
sections.body: The desktop layout pairs a strong wellness headline with a large product card, ingredient visuals, naturalness claims, a satisfaction marker, and a direct CTA. Each element supports the product pitch instead of decorating around it.

sections.label: WHY IT WORKS
sections.title: It reduces the distance between discovery and decision.
sections.body: Visitors can understand the offer, see the bottle, scan the ingredients, notice trust signals, and choose the next action without waiting for lower sections to do the persuasive work.

sections.label: DESKTOP IMPLEMENTATION
sections.title: A split composition that keeps the product in view.
sections.body: On desktop, the left side carries the core message and CTA while the right side turns the product image into a proof system: bottle, botanicals, ingredient list, organic badge, natural claim, and zero-chemical reassurance.

sections.label: MOBILE ADAPTATION
sections.title: The same story, stacked for thumb-first scanning.
sections.body: On mobile, the hierarchy compresses into a vertical pitch: brand, headline, benefit copy, CTA, social proof, and product card. The product remains visually dominant without burying the action.

screenshots.label: Desktop hero
screenshots.alt: Veganic desktop homepage hero with product pitch
screenshots.caption: The desktop viewport turns the product visual into a conversion asset, not a secondary decoration.

screenshots.label: Mobile hero
screenshots.alt: Veganic mobile homepage hero with product pitch
screenshots.caption: The mobile version preserves the same conversion sequence while stacking the story for faster scanning.

decisions: Lead with the product promise before broader brand storytelling.
Use the product card to combine bottle, ingredients, claims, and reassurance.
Surface social proof in the first viewport instead of hiding it below the fold.
Make the CTA a direct bridge from homepage attention to PDP consideration.
Keep desktop and mobile aligned around the same product-first narrative.

journey.label: HOMEPAGE TO PDP JOURNEY
journey.body: The hero introduces the product's desired outcome, validates the formula with ingredient and naturalness cues, builds confidence through social proof and safety markers, then sends the visitor toward the PDP with less uncertainty.

takeaway.label: STRATEGIC TAKEAWAY
takeaway.body: For a single-product store, the hero should not be a decorative entry point. It should behave like the first chapter of the product page, compressing discovery, trust, and consideration into one focused post-click moment.

items.headline: Turning a complex service into a clear sales journey.
items.summary: ClickNShip needed to explain a high-trust managed e-commerce service without overwhelming first-time visitors. We structured the page as a progressive journey from offer clarity to education, proof, trust, and call booking.
items.problem: The service combines account management, sourcing, listing, pricing, fulfillment, customer service, and eBay retail arbitrage. New visitors needed to understand ownership, transparency, inventory, risk, legitimacy, and the next step.
items.solution: We organized the one-page experience as a guided sales conversation, using a service-led hero, above-the-fold lead capture, educational sections, comparison moments, operational proof, social proof, and repeated call-booking paths.
items.impact: A one-page sales system designed to make a complex service easier to understand and easier to act on.
items.imageAlt: ClickNShip managed eBay store landing page with service benefits and lead form

preview.headline: Turning a complex service into a clear sales journey.
preview.summary: We structured ClickNShip's one-page website as a progressive sales journey, moving visitors from offer clarity and education to proof, trust, objection handling, and call booking.
preview.imageAlt: ClickNShip landing page hero and lead form

detail.title: A one-page sales journey built around one clear action.
detail.introduction: ClickNShip sells one core managed e-commerce service, but the offer requires explanation and trust before a visitor is ready to book a call. The page was structured as a complete sales journey, not a generic corporate website.

sections.label: PROJECT INTRODUCTION
sections.title: One service, one page, one primary conversion.
sections.body: The experience centers the entire site around a single business objective: helping visitors understand ClickNShip's managed eBay retail arbitrage service and confidently move toward booking a call.

sections.label: THE CHALLENGE
sections.title: A complex offer that needed immediate clarity.
sections.body: Potential customers need to understand what ClickNShip manages, what the client owns, how the model works, whether inventory is required, how transparency is handled, and why booking a call is the right next step.

sections.label: STRATEGIC INSIGHT
sections.title: The page needed to behave like a guided sales conversation.
sections.body: Because ClickNShip sells one primary service, a focused one-page structure was more appropriate than sending visitors through disconnected pages. Each section answers the next logical question in the decision journey.

sections.label: FIRST VIEWPORT
sections.title: Clarity and conversion above the fold.
sections.body: The hero combines a direct service-led headline, immediate benefits, a lead form, and a Book a Call path. The form is part of the sales architecture: it reduces the distance between interest and action.

sections.label: EDUCATION
sections.title: Teaching the model before asking for commitment.
sections.body: The page introduces educational content early so visitors can understand retail arbitrage, the operating model, and the sections that follow without relying on marketing claims alone.

sections.label: PROCESS
sections.title: Turning the service into a simple operating flow.
sections.body: The service becomes easier to trust when the workflow is visible. Product sourcing, listing and pricing, order fulfillment, and customer service are broken into clear stages.

sections.label: VALUE FRAMEWORK
sections.title: Building the offer around the metrics that matter.
sections.body: Reviews, rating, and traction turn abstract operational goals into a simple framework that helps visitors understand what the service is designed to improve.

sections.label: DIFFERENTIATION
sections.title: Making the difference easy to compare.
sections.body: Comparison sections make the positioning concrete by addressing account ownership, fee transparency, contract flexibility, dashboard access, inventory risk, product sourcing, and exit freedom.

sections.label: OBJECTION HANDLING
sections.title: Addressing risks before the sales call.
sections.body: The page compares traditional inventory operations with retail arbitrage to address concerns around capital tied up in inventory, warehouse costs, shipping responsibility, catalog limits, and operational overhead.

sections.label: PROOF AND TRUST
sections.title: Operational proof inside the decision path.
sections.body: Dashboard visuals support legitimacy without making unverified performance claims. The page keeps proof and trust inside the conversion path.

screenshots.label: Hero and lead capture
screenshots.alt: ClickNShip managed eBay store landing page with service benefits and lead form
screenshots.caption: The first viewport introduces the offer, benefits, lead form, and primary call-booking path in one focused entry point.

screenshots.label: Lead form complete
screenshots.alt: ClickNShip call booking form displayed inside the landing page hero
screenshots.caption: The above-the-fold form reduces the distance between qualified interest and action.

screenshots.label: Operating process
screenshots.alt: Four-step ClickNShip retail arbitrage service process
screenshots.caption: The workflow is broken into sourcing, listing, fulfillment, and customer service so the service feels easier to understand.

screenshots.label: Value framework
screenshots.alt: ClickNShip framework focused on reviews, rating and traction
screenshots.caption: The page translates operational goals into a simple framework visitors can remember.

screenshots.label: Comparison table
screenshots.alt: ClickNShip service comparison table showing ownership, transparency and access differences
screenshots.caption: The comparison makes differentiation concrete across ownership, transparency, dashboard access, inventory risk, and flexibility.

screenshots.label: Operational proof
screenshots.alt: Grid of ClickNShip eBay sales and operational dashboard screenshots
screenshots.caption: Operational proof is integrated into the journey as visible context before the booking action.

screenshots.label: Inventory comparison
screenshots.alt: Comparison between traditional inventory and retail arbitrage operating models
screenshots.caption: Objection handling explains why the model may reduce certain operational burdens without promising financial outcomes.

screenshots.label: Mobile booking form
screenshots.alt: Mobile ClickNShip booking form with first name, last name, email, phone and schedule call button
screenshots.caption: The mobile booking flow keeps the form, consent step, and Schedule My Call action in one focused conversion card.

screenshots.label: Mobile proof section
screenshots.alt: Mobile ClickNShip proof in the numbers section with dashboard screenshots and 90 days tab selected
screenshots.caption: The proof section adapts operational dashboard visuals into a stacked mobile layout with clear section hierarchy.

decisions: Use one-page architecture for one primary service instead of fragmenting the journey across pages.
Pair the hero promise with above-the-fold lead capture and a persistent Book a Call action.
Introduce education before asking visitors to evaluate the offer deeply.
Use comparison sections to address objections with concrete criteria.
Integrate operational proof and social proof before the final action.

journey.label: CLARITY TO CALL BOOKING
journey.body: The journey moves visitors through clarity, education, differentiation, proof, trust, and action. Each section has a specific role in helping the visitor understand the service before committing to a call.

takeaway.label: STRATEGIC TAKEAWAY
takeaway.body: When a business sells one complex service, the strongest website is often not the one with the most pages. It is the one that presents the right information in the right order.

before: Campaign promise breaks on the landing page
CTA buried behind friction and hesitation
Mobile journeys that leak high-intent traffic
Weak proof, weak trust, weak buying confidence
Testing focused on opinions instead of revenue
Paid traffic scaling into preventable drop-off
after: Optimized pages aligned with campaign intent
A/B tests with strong hypotheses and clean tracking
Sharper offers, CTAs, and page hierarchy
Mobile-first flows that protect buying momentum
AI used properly to accelerate research and iteration
A system that turns traffic into measurable revenue

experiments.hypothesis: Reordering social proof above the CTA will increase checkout starts on mobile.
A product-focused headline outperforms a benefit-led headline for warm retargeting traffic.
Removing navigation from the landing page will reduce exit rate and increase conversions.

reasons: Strategy and execution in one team
No generic templates — built for your traffic
Attention to detail at every layer

faq.question: What does post-click optimization mean?
faq.answer: It means improving every experience after a person clicks an ad, email, social post, or search result — from the landing page through checkout.

faq.question: Do you use AI in the process?
faq.answer: Yes. We use AI for research, pattern detection, concept generation, copy exploration, and testing velocity. The difference is that every AI-assisted decision is filtered through conversion strategy, brand fit, user behavior, and measurable outcomes.

faq.question: Is PCL only for e-commerce brands?
faq.answer: No. We also support SaaS, subscription, lead-generation, and service businesses with measurable digital conversion goals.

faq.question: Can you design and develop the full page?
faq.answer: Yes. Strategy, copy direction, UX/UI design, development, quality assurance, and launch can all be handled within one workflow.

faq.question: Do you run A/B tests?
faq.answer: Yes. We define hypotheses, build variations, implement tracking, launch tests, and turn the results into the next optimization cycle.

faq.question: Can you work with our internal marketing team?
faq.answer: Yes. PCL can operate as an embedded post-click specialist alongside your acquisition, brand, product, and engineering teams.

faq.question: Do you offer ongoing optimization?
faq.answer: Yes. Ongoing partnerships are designed as continuous research, experimentation, design, and development cycles.

faq.question: How long does a project take?
faq.answer: Timing depends on scope. A focused landing page can move quickly, while broader funnels and experimentation programs require a longer engagement.

faq.question: Can you improve an existing website?
faq.answer: Yes. We can audit and optimize an existing experience without rebuilding everything from scratch.

footer.description: Post-click growth agency. We optimize everything that happens after the click through strategy, design, development, experimentation, and smart use of AI where it improves conversion.
